page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 16 dedicated physical cores using the Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core with a single thread per core allocation.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring reliance on external block storage volumes for persistent data. It also lacks dedicated GPU hardware accelerators. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

The dedicated CPU allocation model ensures that each of the 16 physical cores operates without resource contention from other tenants, supporting predictable execution times. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance profile qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require local disk storage or GPU acceleration. Suitable workloads include in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and memory-heavy data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Running on the KVM hypervisor, the instance allocates dedicated CPU resources with one thread per core, ensuring consistent compute performance. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local storage and GPU accelerators, meaning all storage must be attached externally. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications that require significant memory capacity relative to compute resources without the need for local high-speed disk arrays.
